task.title: Setting up the bridge on the authenticator

li.p: The server has multiple Ethernet interfaces.

context.p: A network bridge is a link-layer device that forwards traffic between hosts and networks based on a table of MAC addresses. If you set up RHEL as an 802.1X authenticator, add both the interfaces on which to perform authentication and the LAN interface to the bridge.

step.cmd: If the bridge interface does not exist, create it:

info.codeblock: # nmcli connection add type bridge con-name br0 ifname br0

step.cmd: Assign the Ethernet interfaces to the bridge:

info.codeblock: # nmcli connection add type ethernet port-type bridge con-name br0-port1 ifname enp1s0 controller br0 # nmcli connection add type ethernet port-type bridge con-name br0-port2 ifname enp7s0 controller br0 # nmcli connection add type ethernet port-type bridge con-name br0-port3 ifname enp8s0 controller br0 # nmcli connection add type ethernet port-type bridge con-name br0-port4 ifname enp9s0 controller br0

step.cmd: Enable the bridge to forward extensible authentication protocol over LAN (EAPOL) packets:
Disable the Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) on the bridge device:
Configure the connection to automatically activate the ports:

li.p: Display the link status of Ethernet devices that are ports of a specific bridge:
li.codeblock: # ip link show master br0 3: enp1s0: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 1500 qdisc fq_codel master br0 state UP mode DEFAULT group default qlen 1000 link/ether 52:54:00:62:61:0e brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff ...

li.p: Verify if forwarding of EAPOL packets is enabled on the br0 device:
If the command returns 0x8, forwarding is enabled.
